commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the branch is conflicting and the remaining blocker is maintainer UX/product acceptance rather than a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the new DB reads are parameterized sqlc output and the added dependency is limited to Postgres array binding. (sha=5af7651ce3f0e56652b89589b510cf12ba99e4bf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hydrates thread_state onto root messages, adds persistent thread activity controls and row-click thread opening in the web timeline, refreshes thread summaries from realtime events, updates generated assets/OpenAPI/types, and adds focused store/e2e coverage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature/UX PR rather than a broken existing contract. Source inspection of current main confirms the persistent timeline thread affordance and message-page thread_state hydration are still absent.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the branch is conflicting and the remaining blocker is maintainer UX/product acceptance rather than a narrow automated repair.

target.title: feat(web): surface thread activity in message rows
target.body:
Fixes #28.

## Summary

- hydrate `thread_state` onto root messages returned by channel/DM message pages
- render an always-visible thread affordance on each root message row
- show stronger reply-count/activity chips for roots with thread replies
- allow mouse/touch users to open a thread by clicking non-interactive message row content
- refresh visible thread summaries from new `thread.reply_created` / `thread.state_updated` realtime events without replay-driven fetch bursts

## Scope / non-goals

This is intentionally focused on thread visibility and message-row thread opening.

Non-goals:
- no browser/PWA notification changes
- no Pushover changes
- no agent.progress/activity changes
- no change to adapter reply routing or thread persistence semantics
- no keyboard-row takeover; keyboard access remains on the actual thread button/chip

## AI assistance transparency

This PR was AI-assisted by PollyBot/Codex. I reviewed the code path, added focused regression coverage, ran local validation, and tested the behavior in a real ClickClack ops-room deployment. Local `codex review` was attempted but could not complete due provider/tooling limits: default `gpt-5.5` lacked project access, `gpt-5.4` returned quota exceeded, and the spark alias was rejected by the CLI provider. I did not count those failed attempts as review coverage.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  - Closed thread replies were easy to miss because root messages had no always-visible thread activity signal, and opening a thread required hitting the hover action or exact thread affordance.
- Real environment tested:
  - Local ClickClack ops-room deployment on macOS via LaunchAgent, served at `127.0.0.1:8803`, using the existing SQLite ops-room data with real thread replies.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Rebuilt and redeployed the local ops-room binary/web bundle.
  - Opened the real ClickClack web app in Chromium/Chrome through Playwright with an existing user session.
  - Located a message row with `.thread-hint.has-replies`.
  - Clicked the message body text, not the thread chip.
  - Verified the thread panel opened for the same root message id.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

Screenshot proof from the real ops-room dark-mode deployment (1905x1271):

![Thread activity hints in dark mode](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PollyBot13/clickclack/pollybot/thread-activity-hints/docs/proof/thread-activity-hints-dark.png)

Click-through smoke output from the same proof pass:

```json
{
  "clickedMessageID": "msg_01kv691kxaydb9cpzazx7t2x7d",
  "openedRootID": "msg_01kv691kxaydb9cpzazx7t2x7d",
  "ok": true,
  "threadHeader": "Thread"
}
```

- Observed result after fix:
  - Thread chips are visible in the main timeline.
  - Clicking non-interactive message text opens the thread panel for that message.
  - Existing interactive targets remain protected: links, buttons, inputs, attachments/media, markdown images/GIF controls, hover actions, failed-message controls, and text selection do not trigger row-open behavior.
- What was not tested:
  - Real Postgres runtime deployment. Postgres store code is covered by the Postgres smoke test when `CLICKCLACK_POSTGRES_TEST_DSN` is available, and the focused local test run exercised the package without a DSN.
  - Mobile hardware tap proof. The implementation uses normal click handling and was tested through Chromium; no iOS/Android device recording is included.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  - The real ClickClack ops-room also carries unrelated local experiment patches. The branch itself was built from a clean worktree based on current upstream `origin/main`, and validation below was run there.

## Validation

Clean upstream worktree at head `059041b`:

- `git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD`
- `go test ./apps/api/internal/store/sqlite ./apps/api/internal/store/postgres ./apps/api/internal/httpapi`
- `pnpm --filter @clickclack/web typecheck`
- `pnpm lint`
- `pnpm fmt:ts:check`
- `pnpm fmt:go:check`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/chat.spec.ts tests/e2e/routing.spec.ts`

Focused behavior coverage added:

- SQLite message-page regression asserts root messages include hydrated `thread_state` with reply counts.
- Postgres smoke test asserts message pages expose hydrated `thread_state` when a thread reply exists.
target.author: pollybot13
